rules.message: Plain-http URL literal ‚Äî mixed content/MITM; use https. Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule ‚Äî same convention as this pack's `localhost-egress-committed`). Suppress a vetted case with `// mixed-content-ok`.

Committed localhost/private-IP endpoint ‚Äî breaks outside the dev machine; move to env/config. If this file is dev-only, exclude it via config instead. Test/e2e/fixture paths (`e2e/`, `tests?/`, `__tests?__/`, `spec/`, `fixtures?/`, `testing/`, or a `*.test.*`/`*.spec.*`/`*-spec.*`/`*.e2e-spec.*` file) are excluded outright, as are test-runner/dev-tool config files anywhere in the tree by basename (`playwright.config.*`, `vitest.config.*`, `jest.config.*`, `cypress.config.*`, `vite.config.*`) ‚Äî a localhost target in a Playwright/Vitest/Jest/Cypress/Vite config or an e2e fixture (including a NestJS-style `*.e2e-spec.ts` or a `packages/testing/` helper) is the intended target, not a leaked dev endpoint. Three further non-egress shapes are excluded by value/context shape rather than path: (1) the localhost literal is a `||`/`??`/ternary `?` fallback alongside `process.env`/`getEnv(`/`IS_PRODUCTION` on the same line ‚Äî an env-override default IS the recommended remedy this rule would otherwise ask for, so it's already applied, not a leak (this only catches the common `env-check-then-operator` line order; a fallback written in the reverse order on the same line is a documented residual gap); (2) `new URL(x, "http://localhost")` ‚Äî the second argument to the WHATWG `URL` constructor is a dummy base used only to parse a relative path, never an egress target; (3) an equality/inequality comparison (`=== "http://localhost:3000"` / `!== 'http://localhost'`) ‚Äî the literal is a comparison sentinel, not a call target. `skip_comment_lines` skips a full-line `//` comment and any `/* ... */`-block line whose trimmed text starts with `*` or `/*` (e.g. JSDoc-style continuation lines) ‚Äî but a block comment whose continuation lines have no leading `*` (a bare `/*\nhttp://localhost:3000\n*/` with no per-line `*` prefix) still has its inner line scanned, a residual gap of this line-local comment heuristic (no multi-line block-comment-state tracking). Suppress a vetted case elsewhere with `// localhost-ok`.

A GET request carrying a body (`method: 'get'` alongside a `body:` property in the same function) ‚Äî servers/proxies may silently drop the body on a GET, so data the caller thinks it's sending never arrives. Use POST (or another body-bearing method) or move the data to query params instead. Comment lines are skipped via `skip_comment_lines`. Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Precision limit: method-scan only proves a GET method literal and a `body:` property co-occur in the same function body, not that the `body:` belongs to the same request-options object as the `method:` ‚Äî an unrelated `body:` elsewhere in the same function also co-triggers. Suppress a vetted case with `// get-body-ok`.

WebSocket opened/upgraded (`new WebSocket(...)`/`.upgrade(...)`) with no auth material (`token`/`auth`/`session`/`cookie`/`jwt`) visible anywhere in the same function ‚Äî an unauthenticated realtime channel lets anyone who can reach the socket connect. Attach a token/cookie/session credential when opening or upgrading the connection. Comment lines are skipped via `skip_comment_lines`. Test/e2e/fixture paths are excluded outright (deployed-surface rule). Precision limit: high-FP heuristic ‚Äî `absent` only checks the whole enclosing function body for those words, not that any auth material found actually secures this specific connection (an auth header set elsewhere in a shared helper, or credentials read from a cookie the browser attaches automatically, are both invisible to this scan); starts at info accordingly. Suppress a vetted case with `// ws-auth-ok`.
